Note: The translation attempts to convey the original meaning, but some phrases may not be perfectly idiomatic in Czech. The concept of "flowers and leaves for the skin" as a cream is a bit unusual, so I translated it as "květiny a listy pro kůži, jako na bázi," which means "flowers and leaves for the skin, as a base." The idea of flowers to lighten hair is translated as "květiny k zlepšení vlaku," which means "flowers to lighten the hair."
